name: Vijayindra Tirtha

dates_evidence.description: Royal-court records of the late Vijayanagara period.

core_thesis: Vijayīndra Tīrtha is the principal late-Vijayanagara consolidator of Vyāsatīrtha's *Navya-Dvaita*, remembered for an extraordinarily prolific polemical corpus traditionally numbering more than a hundred works (the round figure of 104 is canonical). His doctrinal commitments are uniformly Mādhva — *svatantra-paratantra* taratamya, *pañca-bheda*, *viśeṣa*, *bimba-pratibimba*, the rejection of *mithyātva* under all five canonical *lakṣaṇas* — but his contribution is the systematic *digvijaya*-style extension of the school's polemic across all the rival darśanas it had not yet fully engaged: Viśiṣṭādvaita (most prominently the lengthy *Madhva-Tantra-Mukha-Mardana*, replying to Appayya Dīkṣita's *Madhva-Tantra-Mukha-Bhañjana*), Śrīkaṇṭha-Śaivism, Nimbārka, Vallabha-line *śuddhādvaita*, the various *Dharmaśāstra* and *Mīmāṃsā* opponents who challenged Mādhva ritual interpretation, and the Navya-Naiyāyikas successive to Gaṅgeśa. Methodologically he extends Vyāsatīrtha's *Navya-Nyāya* technique into ever-finer-grained *vyāpti*-analysis, especially in disputed inferences over *bheda*, *sākṣi-pramāṇic* status, and the *jīva*-classification *taratamya*. He is the bridge from Vyāsatīrtha to Rāghavendra — many of Rāghavendra's *Pariṃala*-passages presuppose Vijayīndra's prior dialectical work.

engaged_works.title: Nyayadhva-Dipika
engaged_works.summary: The *Nyāyādhva-Dīpikā* ("Lamp on the Path of Reasoning") is one of Vijayīndra's central methodological *prakaraṇa*s — an exposition of the Mādhva doctrine of *pramāṇa* and *prameya* in *Navya-Dvaita* form, set as a *dīpikā* on the *nyāya* (here *yukti*-*paddhati*) by which the school proceeds. The work fixes the school's analysis of *anubhava*-*sākṣi* as a self-luminous *pramāṇa*, the conditions under which *anumāna* is and is not subject to *bādha*, and the *vyāpti*-conditions specific to inferences in which the *sādhya* is itself a relational property (e.g. *bheda*, *taratamya*, *bimbatva*). Vijayīndra here also defends, against Appayya-line and Vivaraṇa critics, the legitimacy of the Mādhva use of disputed *Pañcarātra* and *khila*-citations in *śabda-pramāṇa*, by arguing that *āpta-vākya* status is a function of the speaker's epistemic standing and not of the conventional acceptance of the textual corpus. The *Nyāyādhva-Dīpikā* is the natural complement to his polemical works: where they deploy the Mādhva method against opponents, this text articulates the method itself.

engaged_works.title: Madhva-Tantra-Mukha-Mardana
engaged_works.composition_dates_notes: Composed in direct response to Appayya Dīkṣita's Madhva-Tantra-Mukha-Bhañjana, locating the work in the late Vijayīndra period.
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored polemic; the canonical Mādhva reply to Appayya Dīkṣita.

ascription_evidence.description: Stable manuscript transmission within the Mādhva maṭha system.

engaged_works.summary: Vijayīndra's lengthy and systematic reply to Appayya Dīkṣita's *Madhva-Tantra-Mukha-Bhañjana* ("Smashing the Face of Madhva-Doctrine") — Appayya having mounted, from a Śivādvaita-inflected Advaita position, a sustained attack on Madhva's authenticity as a Vedāntin and on his use of disputed scriptural citations. The *Madhva-Tantra-Mukha-Mardana* ("Crushing the Face that Smashes Madhva-Doctrine") proceeds objection by objection: (i) defending the *prāmāṇya* of the disputed *Pañcarātra*, *khila*, and *Bhāratīya*-affiliated citations on which Madhva's positive theology of Viṣṇu-as-supreme rests, by arguing that *āpta-vākya* status is fixed by the speaker's epistemic standing not by conventional textual canon; (ii) rebutting Appayya's specific *adhikaraṇa*-readings of the *Brahma-Sūtra* in which Śiva is identified as *param-Brahman*; (iii) refuting the Śivādvaita strategy by which Appayya seeks to absorb apparent Vaiṣṇava *śruti* under a higher Śaiva-monist key. The text is the principal late-medieval Mādhva engagement with Śivādvaita and the work in which Vijayīndra most clearly extends the school's polemic beyond its earlier Advaita and Viśiṣṭādvaita targets.

engaged_works.title: Nyayamrta-Vyakhya
engaged_works.composition_dates_notes: Composed across Vijayīndra's mature period in dialogue with the developing Madhusūdana-line response to the Nyāyāmṛta.
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored commentary on Vyāsatīrtha's Nyāyāmṛta.
engaged_works.summary: Commentary on Vyāsatīrtha's *Nyāyāmṛta* — the *Navya-Dvaita* magnum opus that gave the school's anti-Advaita polemic its definitive *Navya-Nyāya* form. Vijayīndra's *Vyākhyā* both clarifies and extends the *Nyāyāmṛta*'s arguments, working *parichcheda* by *parichcheda* through Vyāsatīrtha's refutation of the Advaitic doctrines of *mithyātva*, *bheda-niṣedha*, *avidyā*, *anirvacanīya-khyāti*, and *jīvanmukti*. Where Madhusūdana's *Advaita-Siddhi* had begun to appear as the formal Advaitic counter, Vijayīndra anticipates and rebuts the new objections in the *Vyākhyā*, maintaining the *Nyāyāmṛta*'s position with refined *vyāpti*-analysis and tighter *Navya-Nyāya* formulations. The text is the principal bridge between Vyāsatīrtha and Rāghavendra's later *Tarka-Tāṇḍava-Pariṃala* on the Naiyāyika side and *Nyāya-Sudhā-Pariṃala* on the *Anuvyākhyāna* side: many of Rāghavendra's settled positions presuppose Vijayīndra's prior dialectical work in this *Vyākhyā*. Together with the *Madhva-Tantra-Mukha-Mardana* it constitutes the spine of Vijayīndra's polemical output on the school's two most active fronts (Advaita and Śivādvaita) in the late sixteenth century.
